key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal neural network can jointly predict laser weld penetration state, depth, and seam morphology from weld pool images and process parameters.
engineering_relevance: The approach supports in-situ quality control for laser welding by enabling faster, more reliable monitoring of weld quality during production.
method: Uses a CNN + state-space model to extract spatial and temporal features from top weld pool images.
Fuses image features with welding parameters in a multi-task prediction framework.
Builds a curated dataset to improve robustness and generalization.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% weld cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of the captured weld-pool imagery, so transfer to new materials, setups, or camera conditions may require revalidation.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
